title: Psalms 64
subtitle: Hear Me, My God, as I Voice My Complaint

sections.header: Verses 1–6 — “They Sharpen Their Tongues Like Swords”

heb.paragraph: "They sharpen their tongues like swords and aim cruel words like deadly arrows (ḥēṣ)" (v.3). The enemies’ weapon is speech: ambush from hiding (v.4), sudden attack (v.4), secret plots (v.5). "Who will see it?" they say (v.5) — the assumption that God is blind to covert evil.

notes.note: "Hide me from the conspiracy of the wicked. They sharpen their tongues like swords and aim cruel words like arrows. They shoot from ambush at the innocent." Speech-as-warfare: words are swords and arrows launched from concealment.
"They say, ‘Who will see it?’ They plot injustice and say, ‘We have devised a perfect plan!’" The arrogance of secret evil: they believe God cannot see.

sections.header: Verses 7–10 — “God Will Shoot Them with His Arrows”

heb.paragraph: "But God will shoot them (yōrēm) with his arrows; they will suddenly be struck down" (v.7). The boomerang: the arrow-shooters are shot. Their own tongues turn against them (v.8). All people will fear and proclaim God’s works (v.9). The righteous rejoice and take refuge (v.10).

notes.note: "God will shoot them with his arrows. He will turn their own tongues against them." The arrow-aimers are arrow-struck. The tongue-sharpeners are cut by their own speech. Justice uses the enemy’s own weapons.
"All people will fear; they will proclaim the works of God. The righteous will rejoice in the LORD and take refuge in him." The outcome: universal fear → proclamation → righteous joy.

ppl.name: God
ppl.text: "They say, ‘Who will see it?’ They plot injustice and say, ‘We have devised a perfect plan!’" The arrogance of secret evil: they believe God cannot see…

chapter_panels.trans: VersionTranslationNIVThey plot injustice and say, “We have devised a perfect plan!” Surely the human mind and heart are cunning.ESVThey search out injustice, saying, \u201cWe have accomplished a diligent search.\u201d For the inward mind and heart of a man are deep.NIVBut God will shoot them with his arrows; they will suddenly be struck down.ESVBut God shoots his arrow at them; they are wounded suddenly.

rec.text: ""Hide me from the conspiracy of the wicked. They sharpen their tongues like swords and aim cruel words like arrows. They shoot from ambush at the innocent." Speech-as-warfare: words are swords and arr…"

rows.label: Tongue Arrows — 64:1–6
rows.text: Swords and arrows of speech. "Who will see it?" Secret plots.

hebtext.note: "They sharpen their tongues like swords and aim cruel words like deadly arrows (ḥēṣ)" (v.3). The enemies’ weapon is speech: ambush from hiding (v.4), sudden attack (v.4), secret plots (v.5). "Who will see it?" they say (v.5) — the assumption that God is blind to covert evil.

"But God will shoot them (yōrēm) with his arrows; they will suddenly be struck down" (v.7). The boomerang: the arrow-shooters are shot. Their own tongues turn against them (v.8). All people will fear and proclaim God’s works (v.9). The righteous rejoice and take refuge (v.10).

themes.note: Justice peaks: the arrow-aimers are arrow-struck. Judgment: God uses the enemy’s own weapons. Sovereignty: nothing is hidden from God.
